skill.name: generate-output
skill.description: Generate complete deliverables such as code, documentation, tests, and written content from validated requirements and saved project standards.
skill.summary: Turns validated requirements into complete, standards-aligned deliverables.
skill.author: AbeJitsu

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Manual review found these are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, template placeholders, and normal instructional text; no executable shell commands, cryptographic operations, reconnaissance behavior, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempts were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Reported as External Commands
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown code fences and example formatting. They do not invoke a shell, interpolate user input, or run commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown documentation and a fenced output template. I found no executable command call or command injection path in this file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Prose
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations contain ordinary description text, test guidance, output placeholder text, success criteria, and an example heading. They do not contain cryptographic algorithms or API calls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of every cited line found no MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptographic usage. The signal appears to be text matching inside normal Markdown content.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern in Guidance
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance location tells the assistant to use anti-pattern guidance when uncertain. It does not request host enumeration, environment inspection, network discovery, or system metadata collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is a process note about avoiding user-defined anti-patterns. I found no evidence of system reconnaissance intent or behavior.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose time turning approved requirements into complete work products. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to create code, tests, documentation, or content that follows saved standards.
actual_capabilities: Transforms validated requirements into complete code, documentation, tests, refactors, or content.
Uses project standards and common patterns when they are available.
Provides a structured deliverable with summary, applied principles, included patterns, and next steps.
Gives project-type guidance for code features, documentation, refactoring, test suites, and content creation.
Encourages self-checking output against the user's stated principles and patterns.
limitations: It depends on prior requirement validation and does not define a validation process itself.
It references a StandardsRepository interface but does not include that implementation.
It does not run tests, build commands, or verification tools by itself.
It provides broad guidance rather than framework-specific implementation rules.

use_cases.title: Build a feature from approved requirements
use_cases.description: Generate complete implementation output after product and engineering requirements are clear.

use_cases.title: Create project documentation
use_cases.description: Produce structured documentation that follows a team template and includes examples or troubleshooting.

use_cases.title: Draft repeatable test coverage
use_cases.description: Create maintainable tests for happy paths, edge cases, setup, and teardown conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a simple deliverable
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one clear output.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the generate-output skill to create the requested deliverable from these validated requirements: [requirements]. Follow best practices and include a short next steps section.

prompt_templates.title: Apply saved project standards
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with team standards available.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the generate-output skill with project type [project type]. Apply these standards and common patterns: [standards]. Create a complete deliverable for: [requirements].

prompt_templates.title: Create tests with conventions
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request focused on test output.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the generate-output skill to write a test suite for [feature]. Include happy paths, edge cases, setup needs, and descriptive test names that match these conventions: [conventions].

prompt_templates.title: Produce a full implementation package
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request combining implementation and support material.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the generate-output skill to create a complete implementation package for [requirements]. Include code, usage notes, tests, applied principles, included patterns, and concrete next steps.

output_examples.input: Validated requirement: Create onboarding documentation for a new API client.
output_examples.output: A structured guide with overview, setup steps, usage examples, troubleshooting notes, related links, and recommended next steps.

output_examples.input: Validated requirement: Add tests for a searchable dropdown component.
output_examples.output: A test plan and test cases covering rendering, search behavior, keyboard navigation, empty states, and expected accessibility behavior.

output_examples.input: Validated requirement: Refactor a helper module using team standards.
output_examples.output: A before-and-after explanation, the refactored deliverable, noted behavior changes, and a summary of standards applied.

best_practices: Validate requirements before using the skill so the generated output has a clear target.
Provide project type, standards, and common patterns when they exist.
Review the final deliverable with tests, formatting, or human review before release.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a substitute for requirement discovery or acceptance criteria definition.
Do not assume referenced standards exist unless they are available in the workspace.
Do not publish generated code or documentation without checking accuracy and project fit.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It generates complete deliverables such as code, documentation, tests, refactoring output, and written content.

faq.question: Does it require project standards?
faq.answer: No. It can use best practices when standards are missing, but standards improve consistency.

faq.question: Can it run tests or builds?
faq.answer: No. The skill gives generation guidance only. Testing and build execution require separate tool use.

faq.question: Is it specific to one programming language?
faq.answer: No. The guidance is language-neutral and depends on the project type and requirements supplied.

faq.question: How should I prepare a prompt?
faq.answer: Provide validated requirements, target project type, relevant standards, and expected output format.

faq.question: Why does the audit mark static findings as false positives?
faq.answer: The cited lines are Markdown examples and prose, not executable commands, cryptographic code, or reconnaissance logic.
